recurringPattern: Dark or glass sidebar with vertically stacked navigation items, each using an outlined icon + label, a bottom section with user avatar and settings icon, and a collapsed-state hamburger or chevron toggle.
designHarm: Unnecessary dark sidebar forces high contrast against a light main area, creating fatigue. The glass variant introduces readability issues on content behind it. The pattern is treated as required when many layouts don't need a persistent sidebar at all.
legitimateExceptions: Desktop-first operating tools where sidebar is the primary command surface (Figma, VS Code)
Apps with deep information architecture that genuinely needs persistent category navigation
possibleFixtures: Standard dark sidebar with icon+label nav items
Light sidebar with contextual visibility instead of persistent display
originalObservations: AI nearly always places a persistent sidebar on the left for any product app or dashboard, even when the navigation has only 3-4 items.
The sidebar-dark, main-light color split is the default rather than a considered choice.
